1.gl: to exist, i.e. be or become, come to pass (always emphatic, and not a mere copula or auxiliary)

1.gl: properly, to incline; by implication, to decline (of the slanting rays of evening); specifically, to pitch atent; gen. to encamp (for abode or siege)
1.kj: abide (in tents), camp, dwell, encamp, grow to an end, lie, pitch (tent), rest in tent.

1.gl: literally, to load up (on the back of man or beast), i.e. to start early in the morning
1.kj: (arise, be up, get (oneself) up, rise up) early (betimes), morning.
1.dv: a primitive root; properly, to incline (the shoulder to a burden); but used only as denominative from H7926 (שְׁכֶם);

1.gl: an encampment (of travellers or troops); hence, an army, whether literal (of soldiers) or figurative (of dancers, angels, cattle, locusts, stars; or even the sacred courts)
1.kj: army, band, battle, camp, company, drove, host, tents.

1.gl: a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of Israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock
properly, hidden, i.e. dark; used only of the north as aquarter (gloomy and unknown)
